does: RoboJailBench introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating adversarial attacks and defenses in embodied AI systems that integrate Vision-Language Models into physical platforms like robots and autonomous vehicles. The work establishes a security taxonomy with 18 categories derived from ISO standards, regulatory rules, and documented incidents, providing structured categories for security violations in embodied agents. The benchmark includes adversarial attack prompts, defense mechanisms, and evaluation metrics that explicitly capture the trade-off between security robustness and the ability to follow benign commands, addressing a critical gap where prior work emphasized attack success rates while ignoring collateral damage to normal operation.
connects: This work builds on recent research in two intersecting areas: jailbreak attacks and defenses for large language models and embodied AI safety. While previous studies introduced jailbreak techniques for chat-based VLMs and some defenses for embodied agents, they relied on fragmented ad-hoc datasets and limited evaluation metrics. RoboJailBench extends this literature by recognizing that adversarial robustness in embodied systems differs fundamentally from text-only models due to physical consequences and real-world safety constraints. It synthesizes insights from both cybersecurity standards and embodied AI safety, connecting traditional information security taxonomies with the unique risks posed by autonomous agents executing commands in physical environments.
enables: This benchmark enables systematic evaluation of defense mechanisms against adversarial attacks in embodied AI, moving beyond anecdotal evidence toward standardized assessment. Future work can leverage RoboJailBench to develop defense algorithms that maintain security against jailbreaks while preserving utility for legitimate commands, a critical research direction for deploying VLM-based robots safely. The security taxonomy and evaluation framework provide infrastructure for comparing defense approaches across different embodied platforms and scenarios, facilitating progress toward robust autonomous systems. The benchmark also enables researchers to identify failure modes in current systems and inform the development of safety-aware training methods for embodied agents operating in real-world settings.
